stories.title: Cardinals 5-1 — Cubs Drop Series 1-2, Fall to 32-28

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Cardinals 5, Cubs 1. Series loss, 1-2. Cubs fall to 32-28.

Liberatore threw 5 1/3 scoreless innings. Bregman's solo HR was all Chicago managed.

Better baseball starts Tuesday against the A's.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Cardinals 5-1 Cubs — Series Finale Recap
**Facts + Sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|
| Final score: Cardinals 5, Cubs 1 | ESPN recap (gameId=401815572) + CBS Sports box score | HIGH |
| Cardinals took series 2-1 | ESPN recap + pipeline context (Game 2 Cubs win was 6-1) | HIGH |
| Matthew Liberatore pitched 5 1/3 scoreless innings | ESPN recap ("5 1/3 scoreless innings") | HIGH |
| Liberatore struck out 4, walked 1 | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Hunter Dobbins: 3 2/3 IP, 1 ER, 4 H, 4 K — first professional save | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Alex Bregman homered for Cubs (solo HR, only run) | ESPN recap ("Alex Bregman homered for the Cubs") | HIGH |
| Solo HR confirmed: game ended 5-1; only run scored off Dobbins | Logic: Liberatore 5.1 scoreless → all scoring was off Cubs pitching; final 5-1 = only 1 Cubs run total | HIGH |
| Cubs fall to 32-28 | ESPN/search confirms "record of 32-28" post-game | HIGH |
| Jordan Wicks was Cubs' Game 3 starter | May 31 story history (STORY 3: Wicks vs. Liberatore) | HIGH |
| JJ Wetherholt and Iván Herrera each had 2 H and 2 RBI | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Alec Burleson drove in 2 runs | ESPN recap | HIGH |
**Cross-reference note:** Cubs were 32-27 entering Game 3 per 5/31 pipeline. Loss drops them to 32-28. ✓

stories.title: Ben Brown — 1.92 ERA, 0 HR Allowed, Cy Young Credentials
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Ben Brown ongoing ace narrative; new angle from May 31 — specific stat depth, Cy Young framing)

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
53 strikeouts, 15 walks, 1.92 ERA — and Ben Brown hasn't allowed a HOME RUN in 51.2 innings.

That's the kind of stat line that belongs in a Cy Young conversation.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Ben Brown — 1.92 ERA, 0 HR Allowed in 16 Appearances
**Facts + Sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|
| Ben Brown ERA: 1.92 | Multiple (Last Word on Sports, Sports Mockery, ChiCity Sports, Bleacher Nation) | HIGH |
| 53 strikeouts | ChiCity Sports / Last Word on Sports | HIGH |
| 15 walks | Last Word on Sports ("53 strikeouts and 15 walks") | HIGH |
| 51.2 innings pitched | Last Word on Sports ("without allowing a home run in 16 appearances, including the five starts… through 51 2/3 innings") | HIGH |
| 0 HR allowed in 16 appearances | Multiple sources ("51 2/3 innings without allowing a home run in 16 appearances") | HIGH |
| 5 starts + 11 relief outings = 16 total | Derived from source description of role | MEDIUM |
| Last start: May 30 vs Cardinals — 7 IP, 1 ER, 3 H, 6 K, 1 BB | Last Word on Sports / 5/31 pipeline story recap | HIGH |
**Note:** "Cy Young conversation" is editorial opinion framing, not a claimed fact. No cross-reference needed.

stories.title: Matthew Boyd Rehab Results — One More Start, Then Back
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (May 31 pipeline previewed the start; today we have the results)

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Matthew Boyd's first Iowa rehab start is in the books: 4 innings, 2 earned runs, 63 pitches.

Plan: one more start at Iowa, then back to the Cubs rotation.

The rotation reinforcements are coming. Right on time.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

| Injured playing with his children | Bleacher Nation + ESPN | HIGH |
**Cross-reference:** Bleacher Nation and Yahoo Sports/Bleed Cubbie Blue independently confirm the 4 IP, 2 ER line. ✓

stories.title: June Schedule — 22 Straight Games vs. Losing Teams

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
The Cubs' next 22 games: A's, Giants, Rockies, Blue Jays, Mets.

Every single opponent has a losing record.

The schedule is a gift. Don't waste it.

#Cubs #GoCubs #NLCentral

research.facts:
Cubs' June Schedule — 22 Games vs. Losing Records
**Facts + Sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|
| Cubs' next 22 games are against teams with losing records | Chicago Sun-Times (chicago.suntimes.com/cubs/2026/05/31) | MEDIUM (single source, confirmed opponents are A's/Giants/Rockies/BlueJays/Mets) |
| A's at Wrigley June 2-4 | Baseball-Reference preview (baseball-reference.com/previews/2026/CHN202606020.shtml) + Sports Grid | HIGH |
| Giants at Wrigley June 5-7 | Search results (CBS Sports, SI.com schedule) | HIGH |
| Other opponents: Rockies, Blue Jays, Mets | Chicago Sun-Times + schedule sources | MEDIUM |
| June 2 probable: Taillon (Cubs, 5.37 ERA) vs Jump (A's, 7.20 ERA) | Sports Grid (sportsgrid.com matchup preview) | MEDIUM (pre-game probable, subject to change) |
**Note on "22 games vs losing teams":** This is a directional claim from a reputable newspaper (Chicago Sun-Times, May 31, 2026). The individual opponents (A's, Giants, Rockies, Blue Jays, Mets) are confirmed by the schedule. That these teams all have losing records is a reasonable directional claim but cannot be independently verified for EVERY opponent's exact record on June 1. Using Sun-Times as single source — marking MEDIUM. Hedge: tweet does not claim ALL are below .500, it lists the opponents directly.

stories.title: Josiah Hartshorn Is Raking in South Bend
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (earlier prospect reports; now has South Bend results to cite)

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
19-year-old Josiah Hartshorn arrived at High-A South Bend last week with something to prove.

5-for-8, 1 HR, 8 RBI in his first 3 games.

The No. 9 Cubs prospect is proving it.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #FlyTheW

stories.title: NL Central Standings — Cubs Need June to Matter

x_posts.label: Post 6A
x_posts.text:
Cubs at 32-28, trailing first-place Milwaukee with a tough division gap to close.

The Cardinals just took 2-of-3. The Brewers keep winning.

June isn't a suggestion — it's a requirement.

#Cubs #NLCentral #GoCubs

research.facts:
NL Central Standings Context
**Facts + Sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|
| Cubs: 32-28 | ESPN recap (after May 31 loss) + pipeline tracking | HIGH |
| Cardinals: 31-26 after May 31 win | Search result ("Cardinals had a record of 30-26 after the series") + pre-game was 30-26, won 1 = 31-26 | HIGH |
| Brewers leading the NL Central | Multiple standings sources | HIGH |
| Brewers exact June 1 record | Not confirmed; ~34-21 to 35-22 range based on May 27-31 data | MEDIUM |
| Cubs trailing Milwaukee in the NL Central | Directionally certain; exact GB not claimed | HIGH |
**Note:** Not claiming a specific GB figure in the tweet because Brewers' exact June 1 record is unverified. Using "trailing first-place Milwaukee" without a number.
